headline: Intel Scrutiny And New Chair Put Governance And Security In Focus
summary: Intel (NasdaqGS:INTC) is under bipartisan U.S. Congressional scrutiny over national security concerns tied to its use of ACM Research chipmaking tools linked to China. Lawmakers are questioning Intel about testing ACM equipment that is associated with Chinese entities subject to U.S. sanctions. At the same time, Intel announced a board leadership transition, with Dr. Craig H. Barratt set to replace longtime chair Frank Yeary. For you as an investor, this brings together two core themes...

headline: Viettel High Tech Accelerates 6G Architecture Leadership through Strategic Global Alliances at MWC 2026
summary: Viettel High Tech, the advanced technology arm of Viettel Group, today announced the establishment of strategic collaborations with Intel, AMD and ID Quantique at Mobile World Congress (MWC) 2026, while formally joining the global 6G alliance initiated by Qualcomm. These agreements mark a strategic milestone in Viettel High Tech's evolution from a large-scale 5G research, development and deployment leader to a co-architect of the global 6G architecture and technology roadmap.

headline: What Intel (INTC)'s Board Transition and AI Partnerships Shift Means For Shareholders
summary: Recently, Intel announced that longtime board chair Frank Yeary will retire after the May 13, 2026 shareholder meeting, with semiconductor veteran Dr. Craig H. Barratt becoming independent chair, while Infosys and Corvex revealed new AI collaborations leveraging Intel platforms and Intel Trust Domain Extensions to secure and scale enterprise and GPU-accelerated workloads. These moves highlight Intel’s push to embed its CPUs, Gaudi accelerators, foundry security features, and 18A roadmap more...

headline: US lawmakers raise concerns about Intel's testing of tools from China-linked firm
summary: A bipartisan group of U.S. lawmakers led by Democratic Senator Elizabeth Warren and Republican Tom Cotton on Wednesday raised national security concerns over Intel's testing of ‌chipmaking tools made by ACM Research, as first reported by Reuters.  ACM Research, California-based producer of chipmaking ‌equipment, has deep roots in China and two overseas units that were targeted by U.S. sanctions.  Its customers include sanctioned Chinese companies such as ​YMTC and SMIC, sparking questions over whether ACM could pass sensitive information from Intel to Chinese customers, the lawmakers said.
